items.title: Parent 'thought it was a joke' upon hearing year 3 NAPLAN tests stolen

children.content: Dozens of primary school students are victims of an analogue data breach after their NAPLAN tests were stolen from a van in Melbourne's inner-north on April Fool's Day.

gallery.altText: two school girls write in a book during class.

thumbnail.altText: two school girls write in a book during class.

items.title: Victoria's anti-corruption watchdog to get follow-the-money powers

children.content: The new powers will allow IBAC to pursue taxpayer funds through subcontracting arrangements and labour-hire companies.

image.altText: Victorian Premier Jacinta Allan in front of flags for a press conference.

items.title: Chadstone cold case accused faces court after 30 years

children.content: A 78-year-old man accused of shooting three people in an Armaguard robbery at a busy cinema in 1994 faces court.

image.altText: Police car next to Armaguard vehicle in Chadstone shopping centre car park.

items.title: Carly grew up in a cult. The experience partly inspired her latest show

children.content: Carly Sheppard is perhaps best known for experimental performance, but a coincidental meeting with a fellow dancer convinced her to go back to her roots.

gallery.altText: Alisdair Macindoe and Carly Sheppard, both early 40s, stand in a field, among farming equipment..

thumbnail.altText: Alisdair Macindoe and Carly Sheppard, both early 40s, stand in a field, among farming equipment..

items.title: Alarm as nursing cuts leave High Country town without after-hours care

children.content: Swifts Creek is an hour-and-a-half drive from the nearest hospital and residents are worried cuts to after-hours care at its Bush Nursing Centre might leave the community at risk.

image.altText: An older woman with a toddler and her mother in a nurse's waiting room

items.title: Melbourne Fringe board rejects artists' request for 'fair cut'

children.content: An open letter to festival organisers that attracted almost 600 signatures asked them to "give artists a fair cut", with specific requests to change the way fees are calculated.

image.altText: A crowed of people under purple and white jellyfish-like lanterns.

items.title: Miley Cyrus emails and emoji texts amid Melbourne film festival data leak

children.content: Melbourne International Film Festival customers receive strange texts and emails after a data breach linked to the event's third-party ticketing system.

gallery.altText: A crowd of people for a Melbourne International Film Festival event.

thumbnail.altText: A crowd of people for a Melbourne International Film Festival event.

items.title: Women abandon football club after sex offender's return

children.content: When a man who sexually assaulted a minor on a post-season football trip was welcomed back to the club with open arms, Megan decided she was done.

image.altText: Half a woman's face. The other half is obscured by an out of focus football

items.title: Barnaby Joyce's link to how e-bikes came to be everywhere

children.content: When Barnaby Joyce was deputy prime minister and transport minister in 2021, his assistant minister Kevin Hogan made a quiet, but hugely significant change to the Road Vehicle Standards Act.

image.altText: A bike with large tyres rides along a public bike path

items.title: Woman charged with joining Islamic State 'renounces' terror group

children.content: Melbourne woman Rayann El Houli appears in court for a bail hearing after being charged with travelling to Syria to join the Islamic State group, before returning home last year.

gallery.altText: A woman wears a black niqab in a court sketch

thumbnail.altText: A woman wears a black niqab in a court sketch

avatar.alt: A man with dark skin in a suit looks at the camera

items.title: Three-year-old child dies in Melbourne house fire

children.content: A child has died in a house fire in Werribee and another person has been taken to hospital with serious injuries.

gallery.altText: A child holds a hose while looking at the camera

thumbnail.altText: A child holds a hose while looking at the camera

items.title: Police urge community caution as man remains at large after Melbourne double stabbing

children.content: Victoria Police are urging people to be cautious after two stabbing incidents across Melbourne, including one where a car was driven into a home in the city's east.

image.altText: A car which has crashed into a house.

items.title: At 15, Mohamed Semra made headlines. At 27, he's making history

children.content: At just 27, Mohamed Semra is not your average mayor. He overcame a childhood stutter and a viral moment in a Melbourne Apple Store to make political history – and he is just getting started.

image.altText: Mohamed Semra wears black mayoral robes and chain while seated at solid timber chambers desk, his hands clasped

items.title: Flotilla activists take evidence of alleged abuse to international court

children.content: Eleven Australians detained by Israeli forces while attempting to deliver aid to Gaza have submitted evidence to the International Criminal Court alleging abuse, torture and other violations during their detention.

image.altText: three australian activists dressed in grey clothes in instanbul airport

items.title: Should politicians be using AI-generated images?

children.content: A Victorian state MP's use of an apparently AI-generated image raises questions about whether AI has any place in politics.

image.altText: Roma Britnell looks at the camera wearing a pink jacket

items.title: 'Stand up with us and for us': Artist's passions collide at surf comp

children.content: Surfer Jessi Rebel's journeys with art and identity have beaten similar paths — both inspired by one relative's sage piece of advice.

gallery.altText: A close-up of Jessi Rebel, pictured at Bells Beach

thumbnail.altText: A close-up of Jessi Rebel, pictured at Bells Beach

items.title: Young motorbike rider critical after crash at motocross event

children.content: A 14-year-old boy has suffered life-threatening injuries in a collision with another motorbike rider at a junior titles event in Victoria's west.

image.altText: A motorbike rider on a dirt track.

items.title: Ski resorts remain optimistic despite forecast for warmer winter

children.content: Alpine business owners and resort managers say they are not overly concerned about a warmer winter forecast, with preparations underway for the 2026 ski season ahead of the King's Birthday long weekend.

image.altText: A ski resort field with several skiiers going down a hill

items.title: AFL concedes umpire error as Cats lament 'howler' call

children.content: Geelong's Ollie Dempsey was denied a clear goal in the narrow loss to Carlton when umpires chose not to call on the AFL Review Centre to check a decision.

image.altText: Ollie Dempsey kicks a goal off his shin

items.title: Former mayor's lawyer describes private prosecution as 'nightmare'

children.content: A private prosecution launched by a businessman in Victoria's Hepburn Shire has been described as a "nightmare" by a lawyer defending one of two local government figures facing charges.

image.altText: A coat of arms sits above a sign that reads Ballarat Law Courts.

items.title: 'Heart ripped out on repeat': Auskick parents say they have been failed by AFL

children.content: The ABC has spoken to several families who say they have been failed by the AFL Integrity Unit following bullying, racism and harassment.

gallery.altText: A shadow of a young boy playing AFL football in his backyard

thumbnail.altText: A shadow of a young boy playing AFL football in his backyard

items.title: Angus Taylor draws 'battle lines' in key Liberal Party address

children.content: Opposition Leader Angus Taylor accuses Prime Minister Anthony Albanese of starting a war on aspiration with the recent federal budget.

image.altText: angus taylor stands in front of liberal party posters and delivers a speech

items.title: Erin Patterson mushroom trial appeal set after 'miscarriage of justice' claim

children.content: Erin Patterson's appeal against her triple murder conviction will be heard in August, Victoria's Supreme Court has confirmed, with the director of public prosecutions also challenging the length of her jail term.

image.altText: Erin Patterson being led into a prison van

items.title: Violent teen on probation before causing fatal Melbourne crash

children.content: A Melbourne teenager who had been on probation for just nine days when he caused a deadly, high-speed crash is also facing court over a bashing that left a man with life-long disabilities.

image.altText: A badly damaged car crashed into a pole.

items.title: Victorian MP sorry for mistakenly saying alleged koala killer had died

children.content: State MP Jacinta Ermacora says "they were misinformed and passed that misinformation on".

image.altText: A koala takes a nap in a tree

items.title: Coroner finds baby's death after home birth was preventable

children.content: A Victorian coroner has found a baby's death could have been avoided if his mother had been transferred to hospital hours earlier, after signs of an obstructed labour.

gallery.altText: A picture of an emergency sign, with carpark, ambulances and windows in background, outside a hospital's Emergency Department

thumbnail.altText: A picture of an emergency sign, with carpark, ambulances and windows in background, outside a hospital's Emergency Department

items.title: Firefighters look to restart PFAS case against manufacturer 3M

children.content: The federal government's $2 billion lawsuit against "forever chemicals" manufacturer 3M has Fire Rescue Victoria considering whether it should relaunch its own legal proceedings.

image.altText: Firefighters spray foam on a structure in a national park

items.title: Women's shed ex-board member banned over alleged transphobic comments

children.content: A former board member of a women's shed in Victoria has been evicted after allegedly "spewing" discriminatory and hurtful comments about transgender people.

image.altText: a close up of a shed and a sign that says 'Women's Shed Ballarat'

items.title: John Farnham says he won't ever sing on stage again ahead of tribute concert

children.content: Jimmy Barnes, Tina Arena and Human Nature will take the stage to honour Australian legend John Farnham at a tribute concert later this year.

items.title: Former Victorian premier's new fence angers neighbours

children.content: A regional Victorian council says it is investigating complaints about 500 metres of fencing that was recently built along former premier John Brumby's property.

image.altText: Man in vest stands in front of intersection.

items.title: People trapped in new housing estate traffic bottlenecks welcome funding

children.content: Motorists in Melbourne's outer north welcome funds for "lifeline" outer suburbs road, but say more needs to be done.

image.altText: Cars queue along road in peak hour traffic with a form one lane sign in the foreground

items.title: Kids protest against McDonald's after fast-food giant buys land in small town

children.content: Children turn out in protest after it's revealed the global fast-food giant has paid $4.6 million for a prominent site in central Victoria's Castlemaine.

gallery.altText: A group of children at a protest

thumbnail.altText: A group of children at a protest

items.title: Officeworks to offshore hundreds of jobs in search for white-collar workers

children.content: The Australian office products retailer is planning to offshore hundreds of jobs to India and the Philippines, joining a growing trend of businesses hoping to find more highly skilled white-collar workers overseas.

image.altText: A large outdoor Officeworks sign in front of large trees.

items.title: Victoria's political donations cap to double to $10,000 under proposed changes

children.content: The cap on political donations in Victoria will be at least doubled and public funding for political parties increased under government proposals to rewrite the regime struck out by the High Court.

image.altText: Looking up at the pillars outside the Victorian Parliament.

items.title: From Torquay to Antarctica, wagtail paintings are popping up everywhere

children.content: A Victorian artist paints hundreds of willie wagtails a week to give away locally and now they're popping up all over the world.

gallery.altText: Mike McLean paints his willie wagtails on pieces of reclaimed timber.
Harper, on her bike, sees a red wagtail painting next to a letterbox.
Two kids point to a green wagtail painting on a fence.

thumbnail.altText: Mike McLean paints his willie wagtails on pieces of reclaimed timber.
Harper, on her bike, sees a red wagtail painting next to a letterbox.
Two kids point to a green wagtail painting on a fence.
